Tests the strict equality comparison between a negated number and a boolean value.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the equality of the expression `!1 === false`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the logical negation of the number `1` results in the boolean value `false`. This test is likely part of a larger suite of tests to validate logical operations or type coercion behavior in JavaScript. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific logical operation and its expected outcome.
